replies.text: Test received! The "Add Comment + Ask Copilot" flow is working — the comment was created, and Copilot chat opened with the context. Did the popover also open automatically for this new comment? That was the fix we just deployed.

Reply "Ask Copilot" flow confirmed working — your "test 2" reply was saved and I can see it in the thread. Both flows are now functional:
1. Add Comment + Ask Copilot ✅
2. Reply + Ask Copilot ✅

Is the popover staying open during these actions now?
Test thread received — your reply "test reply" is saved. This response was triggered via Reply Ask Copilot. Is the popover staying open this time after this agent reply appears?
Full path is now showing in the prompt — confirmed working. Your reply "tsest" was saved and the thread has 3 replies. How's the button sizing and popover behavior looking now?

comments.blockPreview: See the [examples/](examples/) folder for a sample design proposal with threaded

replies.text: Test comment 2 received! This was triggered via Add Comment + Ask Copilot. Did the popover open automatically for this new comment and stay open after this reply?

Markdown Review is a VS Code extension that adds inline commenting and review capabilities to markdown files — similar to what you'd find in Google Docs or Quip. It lets you add comments on specific blocks of a markdown document, reply in threads, and resolve discussions, all without leaving VS Code. It also exposes 7 built-in Copilot tools so AI agents can participate in the review process — reading comments, replying, and resolving them. It's designed for document reviews, design proposals, and technical specs.

comments.comment: I still don't see the picture even after reload. Why is that?

replies.text:
The code fix is correct — the image file exists and the regex/path resolution works. The issue is that the webview panel was created before the `localResourceRoots` fix was deployed, and it's still using the old settings (which only allowed `media/`). 

To fix: **Close the "Review: README.md" tab entirely** (click the × on the tab), then reopen it with Ctrl+Shift+R. Simply reloading the window isn't enough because the webview retains its configuration. A fresh panel will include the document directory in `localResourceRoots`.

I have alerady done that and tried again. The picture is still not displayed in the preview. 

I am not sure if it is because we are currently using the preview in a different repo other than the readme.md file actually lives.

Good point about the different repo. I've added debug logging to `resolveImagePaths`. After reload, close the preview tab, reopen it (Ctrl+Shift+R), then check the **Output** panel → select **"Markdown Review"** from the dropdown. You should see logs like:
- `resolveImagePaths: docDir=...` — shows what directory it's resolving from
- `resolveImagePaths: examples/screenshot.png -> ... -> ...` — shows the webview URI

This will tell us exactly what's happening. If there are no `resolveImagePaths` log lines at all, the regex isn't matching the HTML.

comments.comment: in this section, also add the ask copilot function to tell user they can ask copilot in place.

replies.text: Done — updated the Quick Start "Add Review Comments" section to mention the ✨ Ask Copilot buttons. It now tells users they can click "Ask Copilot" instead of "Add Comment" to get an immediate AI response, or click it next to "Reply" to send the thread to Copilot.
